mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns exceed 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS is above its 10-year median, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the following trading week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following a 0.2pp unemployment jump, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit compared to additive proxies in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns during mid-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns during mid-cycle expansions, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and 20-day returns drops below 0.10 in rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding trend levels reflects strong aggregate demand, which immediately boosts market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 60% of periods following above-trend GDP prints, or the signal exhibits zero predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above the 3.5 percent threshold forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 55% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5 percent, or the threshold effect disappears in rolling validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in more than half of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand, which immediately pressures corporate earnings forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 returns during late-cycle downturns.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines fail to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal produces positive forward returns in over 60% of observed downturn periods.
